notes: Coverage is the last 7 days in Asia/Taipei.
Items are deduplicated against the prior week by id and source URL; this week is a fresh set of six items.

highlights.title: MOHW social-work grant intake hit its June 8 submission gate and local reviews now run into June 16
highlights.why: This creates an immediate follow-up window for any social-work, community, or volunteer-service proposals Kevin may want to track or benchmark.
highlights.next: Check on June 16-17 whether any local-government or partner-facing follow-up materials appear and capture the parts worth reusing for future proposals.

highlights.title: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 9, then suspended access on June 12
highlights.why: The useful signal is not just model capability. It is that workflow reliability and fallback planning still matter even for headline model launches.
highlights.next: Keep any model-specific automation pilot behind a fallback plan and recheck access-restoration status before standardizing a new dependency.

highlights.title: OpenAI shipped a smaller but workflow-relevant Codex update on June 11
highlights.why: Rate-limit reset banking and in-app referrals are small features, but they point to Codex becoming more team-distributed and quota-managed rather than purely solo use.
highlights.next: Decide whether any weekly artifact flow should be tested with a shared Codex handoff pattern instead of file-only delivery.

items.title: MOHW closed the 116-year welfare and social-work lottery-grant intake on June 8
items.oneLine: The Ministry of Health and Welfare's Social Assistance and Social Work Department closed applications for 116-year public-welfare lottery grants on June 8, with local-government review handoff due by June 16.
facts: MOHW says private organizations had to submit proposals by 2026-06-08 through the social-welfare subsidy application system.
The same notice says local governments must complete the local-plan initial-review forwarding step by 2026-06-16.
The eligible plan scope explicitly covers social assistance, disaster relief, community development, social work, volunteer service, and public fundraising.
yourTake: This is a useful operational checkpoint because it marks when 2026 social-work proposal activity moves from intake to review and shortlist preparation.
If Kevin wants to track social-work field priorities, this is a better signal than generic conference talk because it ties directly to fundable project framing.
actions: On or after 2026-06-16, check whether any local-government or partner-facing review notes, shortlist signals, or reused proposal templates become visible.
Capture the strongest recurring plan themes for future FamilyFin or welfare-adjacent proposal drafting.

sources.label: MOHW Social Assistance and Social Work Department - 116-year lottery grant intake notice

credibility.reason: Primary MOHW department notice with concrete intake and forwarding deadlines.

followups: Recheck after 2026-06-16 for review-stage or shortlist signals.

items.title: FSC published the 114-year financial education results booklet on June 10
items.oneLine: The Financial Supervisory Commission added a new financial-education results booklet on June 10, creating a fresh official source to mine for reusable outreach formats and policy framing.
facts: The FSC financial-education booklet page lists a new 114-year results booklet dated 2026-06-10.
The same page shows it as the newest item in the financial-education results series.
This gives Kevin a current official compilation point rather than relying only on single-event campaign pages.
yourTake: The practical value is not the PDF itself, but the chance to extract which education formats the regulator chose to highlight after a full year of work.
This can become a better benchmark set for FamilyFin education design than isolated activity announcements.
actions: Read the new booklet and pull out three reusable outreach or anti-fraud education formats worth adapting.
Note which examples are useful for member education versus policy/context background only.

sources.label: FSC - Financial education results booklet page

credibility.reason: Primary FSC publication index with the new booklet date.

followups: Extract concrete format ideas rather than only summarizing the booklet.

items.title: OpenAI added rate-limit reset banking and referrals to Codex
items.oneLine: The June 11 ChatGPT release notes added Codex rate-limit reset banking and in-app referral invitations for eligible Plus and Pro users.
facts: OpenAI's ChatGPT release notes on 2026-06-11 include a Codex update section.
That update says eligible Plus and Pro users now have banked rate-limit resets, including one free reset at launch.
The same note says users can send referral invitations from within the Codex app and the granted reset remains usable for 30 days.
yourTake: This is a small product update, but it matters for recurring workflows because it nudges Codex toward shared-team usage and quota planning instead of ad hoc solo runs.
If Kevin starts delegating weekly artifacts through Codex more often, usage-budget behavior becomes an operational consideration, not just a product footnote.
actions: Decide whether any weekly artifact handoff should be tested with a shared Codex workflow and note the quota-management implications.
Keep local-state checkpoints as the durable source of truth instead of assuming product-level memory or sharing features cover continuity.

sources.label: OpenAI Help Center - ChatGPT release notes

credibility.reason: Primary OpenAI release notes page for ChatGPT and Codex product changes.

followups: Watch whether later updates expand the same team-sharing pattern beyond referrals and resets.

items.title: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, then suspended access three days later
items.oneLine: Anthropic introduced Claude Fable 5 for general use and Mythos 5 through Project Glasswing on June 9, then posted an access suspension update on June 12.
facts: Anthropic's June 9 announcement launched Claude Fable 5 and described Mythos 5 as an upgrade path through Project Glasswing for a small trusted group.
The same page says Mythos 5 is the same underlying model as Fable 5 with some safeguards lifted for a limited cyber-defense context.
A June 12 update on that page says access to Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 was suspended while Anthropic works to restore service.
yourTake: The capability story matters less than the operational lesson: new model access can still be unstable inside the same week it launches.
This reinforces Kevin's preference for fallback-ready workflows and for not anchoring durable processes to one provider announcement too early.
actions: When comparing models for workflow pilots, include service-stability and fallback-readiness as explicit criteria.
Recheck whether access is restored before using this launch as evidence for any workflow migration.

sources.label: Anthropic - Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5

credibility.reason: Primary Anthropic launch page that also carries the June 12 suspension update.

followups: Watch for restoration timing and any explanation of the access suspension.

items.title: OpenAI reported PRC-linked influence operations targeting US AI debates
items.oneLine: OpenAI said it banned two clusters of ChatGPT accounts linked to apparent covert influence operations focused on AI data-center narratives, tariffs, and false claims about OpenAI data compromise.
facts: The report describes two clusters of ChatGPT accounts likely originating from China that OpenAI banned.
One cluster pushed AI data-center electricity-cost narratives, while another criticized US tariffs and amplified false claims that ChatGPT user data had been compromised.
yourTake: For Kevin's workflows, the relevant signal is governance and provenance discipline: AI-adjacent narratives will increasingly need source-checking because policy and infrastructure debates are becoming influence targets.
This is especially relevant for any future public-facing AI or policy summary artifact.
actions: Keep source freshness and provenance checks explicit whenever a weekly radar item touches AI policy, infrastructure, or geopolitical narratives.
Treat high-velocity AI policy discourse as a category where secondary summaries deserve extra skepticism.

sources.label: OpenAI - PRC-linked influence operations are targeting AI debates in the US

credibility.reason: Primary OpenAI report describing account takedowns and narrative patterns.

followups: Watch whether other model providers publish similar influence-operation disclosures.

items.title: Google expanded Demand Gen with creator boosts, Maps inventory, and broader checkout links
items.oneLine: Google's latest Demand Gen update makes it easier to boost creator videos, distribute product videos dynamically, add Maps inventory, and expand checkout links to more markets.
facts: Google says Demand Gen now supports creator-partnership boosts directly in campaign setup.
The update adds Google Maps inventory and expands checkout links to nine new markets.
Google also says advertisers with large product selections typically see a 33 percent conversion lift when adopting product feeds in Demand Gen campaigns.
yourTake: The transferable lesson for Kevin is not paid media execution itself. It is the tighter loop between trusted creator proof, local discovery, and reduced-click conversion paths.
That pattern can be mimicked in simpler internal or public funnels even without running YouTube campaigns.
actions: Review whether any public-facing project should add a lighter trust-building entry path before asking users to navigate a deep content tree.
Capture one creator-proof or local-discovery pattern that could be adapted into a lower-friction landing flow.

sources.label: Google Blog - Fuel your next wave of growth on YouTube with Demand Gen

credibility.reason: Primary Google announcement describing the new Demand Gen features and rollout framing.

followups: Track which parts of this pattern are easiest to adapt without paid-media dependence.

trackingItems: Recheck MOHW/local-government follow-up signals after the 2026-06-16 review handoff.
Extract three reusable formats from the FSC financial-education results booklet.
Watch Anthropic Fable 5 and Mythos 5 service-restoration status before adopting any workflow dependency.
Keep one creator-proof or lower-friction funnel pattern on watch for reuse.
